articles.title: New Movies Coming Soon: Avengers and More!
articles.summary: At a big movie event called CinemaCon in Las Vegas, Hollywood studios showed exciting new films. The next Avengers movie, Avengers: Doomsday, has a trailer with Iron Man as a new villain. It comes out in December and brings back old heroes like Captain America and Thor. Tom Cruise is making Top Gun 3, and Ariana Grande is in a funny movie called Focker In-Law. This event lets fans see trailers before they go online, and stars like Chris Evans talk about their roles. It helps movie theaters by showing popular films that people want to watch.

articles.title: Fire Attack on a Media Office in London
articles.summary: Three people have been charged for trying to start a fire at a Persian language media office in London. They threw a burning container at the building, but it landed in a car park and the fire went out quickly. No one was hurt, and no damage was done. The police chased a car that crashed, and the three people are now going to court. This happened after other similar attacks in the area, including at a synagogue and on ambulances. The police say there is no bigger danger to the public.

articles.title: Many Cars Taken for No Insurance, Including a Fancy Lamborghini
articles.summary: In the UK, police took nearly 160,000 cars off the roads last year because they did not have insurance, which is the highest number in 17 years. Insurance helps pay if there is an accident, but some people drive without it because it can be expensive. A group called the Motor Insurers' Bureau says about 300,000 cars are driven every day without insurance. In one police operation, they seized 16 vehicles, including a Lamborghini, which is a very expensive sports car. Uninsured driving costs the UK a lot of money, about £1 billion each year, for things like helping victims and medical costs. Places like Birmingham have many accidents with uninsured drivers. The police and the bureau are working together to stop this problem and teach people about the law.

articles.title: A Big Problem for the Prime Minister
articles.summary: This article talks about a big problem for the prime minister, Sir Keir Starmer. He sent Lord Mandelson to Washington, but there was trouble because Lord Mandelson failed a security check. The Foreign Office overruled this, and it caused a lot of upset. Sir Olly Robbins, a top civil servant, lost his job because of this. People in the opposition and even in the Labour Party are angry and think the prime minister might have to resign. The prime minister says he didn't know about the security check failure until later. He will talk to Parliament about it on Monday. Sir Keir is very angry, and many people say they didn't know what was happening. This story shows how mistakes can cause big problems in government.

articles.title: Former NBA Player Damon Jones in Gambling Trouble
articles.summary: Damon Jones, a former NBA player, is expected to plead guilty in a big gambling case. He was arrested with over 30 people, including other basketball figures and mobsters. Jones is accused of cheating in poker games and sharing secret injury information about star players like LeBron James to help people win bets. He had said he was not guilty before but now might change his plea in court. Jones played basketball for many years and earned a lot of money. He was a teammate of LeBron James and later helped coach him. Prosecutors say he texted someone to bet on a game because he knew James was hurt, even though it wasn't public yet. This case shows how gambling can lead to serious legal problems for athletes.

articles.title: Jet Fuel Shortages and Air Travel
articles.summary: A jet fuel shortage is happening in Europe and Asia because of a war and the Strait of Hormuz being closed. This strait is important for bringing jet fuel to Europe. The shortage could mean higher prices for airplane tickets and some flights might be canceled soon, especially as summer travel starts. Jet fuel is a big cost for airlines, making up about 30% of their expenses. Since the war began, jet fuel prices have doubled. Experts say Europe has only about six weeks of jet fuel left. Airlines are trying to keep customers calm, but some are already charging more for bags or tickets. A few airlines have cut flights, and other parts of travel like schedules could change. This affects travelers by making trips more expensive and less reliable.
